chunk_summary: Guidance on household food safety and safe water practices for complementary feeding, focusing on preventing foodborne illness in children 6–24 months through hygiene, safe storage, and proper food preparation techniques.
learning_objectives: Understand the WHO 'Five Keys to Safer Food' and their application in complementary feeding.
Identify high-risk foods and practices for children under five and recommend safer alternatives.
Learn proper food storage, reheating, and hygiene practices to prevent foodborne illnesses.
Promote the use of clean utensils and safe water in food preparation and feeding.
key_facts: Diarrheal disease can rapidly erode growth gains in children 6–24 months.
Discard foods left at room temperature for more than 2 hours, or less in very hot environments.
Handwashing with soap before food preparation and feeding is a high-impact intervention.
Avoid feeding bottles due to contamination risks; use a clean cup and spoon instead.
Children under five are at higher risk of severe foodborne illness from raw or undercooked foods.
Reheat stored food until steaming hot throughout before serving.
reference_quote: "Keep clean; separate raw and cooked; cook thoroughly; keep food at safe temperatures; and use safe water and raw materials." — WHO 'Five Keys to Safer Food'
document_key_topics: High-risk foods for children under five
